rules.title: Horizontal expansion is limited
rules.description: Horizontal expansion is limited
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<SG2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Horizontal expansion is limited.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} cannot expand horizontally beyond the 11” x 20” starting size limit at any time in the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<SG2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Horizontal expansion is limited.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} cannot expand horizontally beyond the 11” x 20” starting size limit at any time in the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Vertical expansion is unlimited
rules.description: Vertical expansion is unlimited
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<SG3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Vertical expansion is unlimited.{{/BOLD}} Once the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may expand vertically beyond the 15” starting size limit with no limits.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<SG3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Vertical expansion is unlimited.{{/BOLD}} Once the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may expand vertically beyond the 15” starting size limit with no limits.

rules.title: Keep Scoring Objects in the Field
rules.description: Keep Scoring Objects in the Field
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Keep Scoring Objects in the Field.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} that leave the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} may be reintroduced by a Loader in accordance with <SG6> with the following restrictions:
  
a. A blue {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} that leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} may only be reintroduced by the blue Loader at the blue {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}; a red {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} that leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} may only be reintroduced by the red Loader at the red {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}.
b. An orange {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#beam}}Beam{{/LINK}} that leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} must be given to the Loader in the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} closest to where it left the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, and can only be reintroduced by that Loader.
c. “Leaving the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}” means that a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is outside of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, no longer in contact with the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} or Field Elements, and not supported by {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} that are still inside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}.
d. If a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is removed from a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} during a <GG10> interaction, it is considered “out of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}” as soon as it is no longer in contact with any {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
e. If a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is on its way out of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} (as determined by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}), but is deflected back into the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} by a {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}}, field monitor, ceiling/wall, or other external factor, <SG4> would still apply. This {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} should be considered “out of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}},” removed by a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, and given to a Loader.
f. If a set of {{LINK:#connected}}Connected{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, they must be separated and reintroduced one at a time by the appropriate Loader(s).
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Note: {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} that are deemed by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} to be intentional or strategic should immediately escalate to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2707}}Q&A 2707{{/LINK}} - Drive Team Members should not leave their Driver Station to retrieve Scoring Objects {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<SG4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Keep Scoring Objects in the Field.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} that leave the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} may be reintroduced by a Loader in accordance with <SG6> with the following restrictions:
  
a. A blue {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} that leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} may only be reintroduced by the blue Loader at the blue {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}; a red {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} that leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} may only be reintroduced by the red Loader at the red {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}.
b. An orange {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#beam}}Beam{{/LINK}} that leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} must be given to the Loader in the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} closest to where it left the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, and can only be reintroduced by that Loader.
c. “Leaving the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}” means that a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is outside of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, no longer in contact with the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} or Field Elements, and not supported by {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} that are still inside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}.
d. If a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is removed from a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} during a <GG10> interaction, it is considered “out of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}” as soon as it is no longer in contact with any {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
e. If a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is on its way out of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} (as determined by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}), but is deflected back into the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} by a {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}}, field monitor, ceiling/wall, or other external factor, <SG4> would still apply. This {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} should be considered “out of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}},” removed by a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, and given to a Loader.
f. If a set of {{LINK:#connected}}Connected{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, they must be separated and reintroduced one at a time by the appropriate Loader(s).
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Note: {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} that are deemed by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} to be intentional or strategic should immediately escalate to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2707}}Q&A 2707{{/LINK}} - Drive Team Members should not leave their Driver Station to retrieve Scoring Objects {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Each Robot gets one Pin as a Preload
rules.description: Each Robot gets one Pin as a Preload
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Each Robot gets one Pin as a Preload.{{/BOLD}} For {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} listed as {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} 1 (printed {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} list) or Red {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} (RobotEvents.com) will use a red {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} 2 / Blue {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will use a blue {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}}. Prior to the start of each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, each {{LINK:#preload}}Preload{{/LINK}} must be placed such that it meets all of the following criteria:
  
a. Contacting exactly one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
b. Not contacting any Field Elements (excluding the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}}), {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}}, or other {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}}.
  If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not present for a Teamwork Match, its {{LINK:#preload}}Preload{{/LINK}} should be placed in the matching {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2869}}Q&A 2869{{/LINK}} - Preloads are required, not optional {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Using the Load Zone
rules.description: Using the Load Zone
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Using the Load Zone.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} may be {{LINK:#load}}Loaded{{/LINK}} through the {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, must meet all of the following criteria:
  
a. Red and blue {{LINK:#pin}}Pins{{/LINK}} may only be Loaded into the {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} that matches the color of that {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#beam}}Beams{{/LINK}} and/or orange {{LINK:#pin}}Pins{{/LINK}} that leave the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} may be reintroduced at either {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} as described in rule <SG4>.
b. The {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} must be placed in contact with the VEX IQ beam attached to the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}}.
c. The Loader may only put a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} into a {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} if no other {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} are in contact with that {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}.
d. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may not contact a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} if that {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is being contacted by a human.
e. Once a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#placed}}Placed{{/LINK}} into a {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} and released, it may no longer be contacted by a Loader.
f. If a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is introduced improperly (e.g., while another {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is in contact with the {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}), it must be retrieved by the Loader and reintroduced.
g. A Loaded {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#beam}}Beam{{/LINK}} must be placed in a stationary position, and must remain in contact with the VEX IQ beam after it is released by the Loader until it is contacted by a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or another {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} that is being manipulated by a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
h. {{LINK:#pin}}Pins{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} outside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} cannot break the plane of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} until the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins. Note: Although it is not required, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are highly recommended to remain some distance away from the {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} until the Loader’s hand has clearly been removed. This will make clauses d & e abundantly clear to {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are responsible for their own actions. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} that involve a Loader and {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} from opposite {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be given to both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}.
• It is expected that most {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule will be accidental. In accidental cases that end up being {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}} (i.e., an illegally-{{LINK:#load}}Loaded{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#connected}}Connected{{/LINK}} to another {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}}), the first occurrence during a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} may be treated as a {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}} and a “final notice” that any future {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} will result in {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
• Any {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} (accidental and intentional) must be treated as a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}.
• Repeated, intentional, or egregious {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} may escalate to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion. One example of an egregious {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} would be placing a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} directly onto a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} without ever contacting the {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2659}}Q&A 2659{{/LINK}} - Match Loads do not have to be placed in vertical positions
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2726}}Q&A 2726{{/LINK}} - Scoring Objects must be Loaded one at a time
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/3020}}Q&A 3020{{/LINK}} - A human Loader can hold as many Pins as they like, and can rearrange Pins in the Driver Station; there’s no required layout for Pins in the Driver Station {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: One Robot per Team
rules.description: One Robot per Team
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}One Robot per Team.{{/BOLD}} Each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} can only bring one (1) {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to a given event. Though it is expected that {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will make changes to their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} at the event, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is limited to only one (1) {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, and a given {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may only be used by one (1) {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. The VEX IQ system is intended to be a mobile robotics design platform. As such, a VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, for the purposes of the VEX IQ Robotics Competition, has the following subsystems:
  
• Subsystem 1: Mobile robotic base including wheels, tracks, or any other mechanism that allows the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to navigate the majority of the flat playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} surface. For a stationary {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, the robotic base without wheels would be considered Subsystem 1.
• Subsystem 2: Power and control system that includes a VEX IQ legal battery, a VEX IQ control system, and associated Smart Motors for the mobile robotic base. Also includes the VEX IQ pneumatic air pump and solenoids if used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
• Subsystem 3: Additional mechanisms (and associated Smart Motors) that allow manipulation of {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} or navigation/manipulation of Field Elements. Given the above definitions, a minimum {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for use in any VEX IQ Robotics Competition event (including Skills Challenges) must consist of subsystems 1 and 2 above. Thus, if you are swapping out an entire subsystem 1 or 2, you have now created a second {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and are no longer legal.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not compete with one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} while a second is being modified or assembled at a competition.
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not have an assembled second {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on hand at a competition that is used to repair or swap parts with the first {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not switch back and forth between multiple {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} during a competition. This includes using different {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} for {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}, and/or {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}.
d. Multiple {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not use the same {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. Once a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has competed under a given {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number at an event, it is “their” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}; no other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may EVER compete with it. The intent of <R1a>, <R1b>, and <R1c> is to ensure an unambiguous level playing field for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are welcome (and encouraged) to improve or modify their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} between events, or to collaborate with other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to develop the best possible game solution.
However, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who brings and/or competes with two separate {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} at the same tournament has diminished the efforts of a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who spent extra design time making sure that their one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can accomplish all of the game’s tasks. A multi-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} organization that shares a single {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has diminished the efforts of a multi-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} organization that puts in the time, effort, and resources to undergo separate individual design processes and develop their own {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
 To help determine whether a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is a “separate {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}” or not, use the Subsystem definitions found in <R1>. Above that, use common sense as referenced in <G3>. If you can place two complete and legal {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} on a table next to each other, then they are two separate {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. Trying to decide if changing a pin, a wheel, or a motor constitutes a separate {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is missing the intent and spirit of this rule.

rules.title: Robots must represent the Team’s skill level
rules.description: Robots must represent the Team’s skill level
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots must represent the Team’s skill level.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be designed, built, and programmed by members of the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are permitted to mentor and teach design, building, and programming skills to the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, but may not design, build, or program that {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. See rules <G2>, <G4>, and <G5>.
  In VIQRC, we expect {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} to teach fundamental {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} principles like linkages, drivetrains, and manipulators, then allow the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to determine which designs to implement and build on their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
 Similarly, {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are encouraged to teach the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} how to code various functions involving applicable sensors and mechanisms, then have the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} program the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} from what they have learned.

rules.title: Robots must pass inspection
rules.description: Robots must pass inspection
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots must pass inspection.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must pass inspection before being allowed to participate in any {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}. Noncompliance with any {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} design or construction rule will result in removal from {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at an event until the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is brought back into compliance, as described in the following subclauses.
  
a. Significant changes to a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, such as a partial or full swap of Subsystem 3, must be re-inspected before the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may compete again.
b. All possible functional {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} configurations must be inspected before being used in competition.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may be asked to submit to spot inspections by {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. Refusal to submit will result in {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}.
d. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is determined to not be legal before a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins and cannot be brought into compliance before the scheduled {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} start time, the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} will be removed from the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may remain at the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} so that the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} does not get assessed a “no-show” (per <GG2>).
e. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} which have not passed inspection (i.e., that are in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of one or more {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules) will not be permitted to play in any {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} until they have done so. <GG3> will apply to any {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} that occur until the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has passed inspection.
f. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has passed inspection, but is later found to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rule during or immediately following a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, then they will be {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} from that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and <R3d>/<GG3> will apply until the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} is remedied and the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is re-inspected. This is the only {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} that will be affected; any prior {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} that have already been completed will not be revisited. <R3d> will apply until the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} is remedied and the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is re-inspected.
g. All inspection rules are to be enforced at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} within a given event. {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} legality at one event does not automatically imply legality at future events. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} which rely on “edge-case” interpretations of subjective rules, such as whether a decoration is “non-functional” or not, should expect additional scrutiny during inspection.
h. Event staff and volunteers are allowed to photograph {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} during inspection and/or at other times as needed.

rules.title: There is a difference between accidentally and willfully violating a Robot rule
rules.description: There is a difference between accidentally and willfully violating a Robot rule
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}There is a difference between accidentally and willfully violating a Robot rule.{{/BOLD}} Any {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules, accidental or intentional, will result in a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} being unable to play until they pass inspection (per <R3d>).
 However, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who intentionally and/or knowingly circumvent or violate rules to gain an advantage over their fellow competitors are in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of the spirit and ethos of the competition. Any {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this sort should be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <G1> and/or the REC Foundation Code of Conduct.
 A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that circumvents a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rule for a competitive advantage should receive an immediate {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} for the current {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and be reported to the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} for discussion with the REC Foundation Regional Support Manager. As a result of that discussion, the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may be {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} from the event. The {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} should also be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.

rules.title: Robots must fit within an 11” x 20” x 15” (279
rules.description: Robots must fit within an 11” x 20” x 15” (279
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots must fit within an 11” x 20” x 15” (279.4mm x 508mm x 381.0mm) volume.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} using more than one possible {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} configuration at the beginning of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} must tell the inspector(s) and have the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} inspected in all configurations. Rule <R3c> will apply if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is placed in an uninspected configuration (i.e., will not be permitted to play until re-inspected, but will not be considered a “no-show”).
b. A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may NOT have its {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} inspected in one configuration and then place it in an uninspected configuration at the start of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Figure R5-1:{{/BOLD}} The approximate size that a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} should start the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in.

rules.title: Officially registered Team numbers must be displayed on Robot License Plates
rules.description: Officially registered Team numbers must be displayed on Robot License Plates
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Officially registered Team numbers must be displayed on Robot License Plates.{{/BOLD}} To participate in an official VEX IQ Robotics Competition Event, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} must first register on RobotEvents.com and receive a VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number.
 This {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number must be legibly displayed on exactly two (2) VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#licenseplate}}License Plates{{/LINK}} on opposing sides of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use the official VEX IQ Robotics Competition License Plate (VEX Part Number 228-7401) or a plain paper version of matching size, such as {{LINK:https://viqrc-kb.recf.org/hc/en-us/articles/28277975906199-Printable-VEX-IQ-Robotics-Competition-License-Plate}}this template in the REC Library{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#licenseplate}}License Plates{{/LINK}} are considered functional components, and must meet the requirements of all {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules.
b. {{LINK:#licenseplate}}License Plates{{/LINK}} must be clearly visible at all times. For example, {{LINK:#licenseplate}}License Plates{{/LINK}} must not be in a position that would be easily obstructed by a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} mechanism during standard {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} play.
c. Additional {{LINK:#licenseplate}}License Plates{{/LINK}} cannot be used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for any purpose.
{{BOLD}}Figure R6-1:{{/BOLD}} A VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#licenseplate}}License Plate{{/LINK}} with a VEX IQ Robotics Competition Team Number written upon it.

{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2814}}Q&A 2814{{/LINK}} - Paper License Plates cannot be attached with tape
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2870}}Q&A 2870{{/LINK}} - Team numbers on legal plates can be stickers or hand-written, but can’t make the plate thicker and have to be clearly legible
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2896}}Q&A 2896{{/LINK}} - Illegal license plate examples, including V5 plates, 2x8 smooth panels, and laminated paper {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Let it go after the Match is over
rules.description: Let it go after the Match is over
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<R7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Let it go after the Match is over.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must be designed to permit easy removal of {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} from their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} without requiring that the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} have power or remote control after the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is over.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<R7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Let it go after the Match is over.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must be designed to permit easy removal of {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} from their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} without requiring that the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} have power or remote control after the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is over.

rules.title: Robots have one Brain
rules.description: Robots have one Brain
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots have one Brain.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are limited to one (1) VEX IQ Robot Brain. Any other microcontrollers or processing devices are not allowed, even as non-functional decorations.
 This includes microcontrollers that are part of other VEX product lines, such as VEX Cortex, VEX EXP, VEXpro, VEX CTE, VEX RCR, VEX V5, VEX GO, or VEX Robotics by HEXBUG*. This also includes devices that are unrelated to VEX, such as Raspberry Pi or Arduino devices.
  
a. If using a first generation VEX IQ Brain, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must use one (1) VEX IQ 900 MHz radio, VEX IQ 2.4 GHz radio, or VEX IQ Smart Radio in conjunction with their VEX IQ Robot Brain. The VEX IQ Brain and VEX IQ Controller may not be physically connected during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and may only communicate through the radio.
b. Additional Robot Brains cannot be used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (even Robot Brains that are not connected).
rules.completeText:

rules.title: Keep the power button accessible
rules.description: Keep the power button accessible
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R9>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Keep the power button accessible.{{/BOLD}} The on/off button on the VEX IQ Robot Brain must be accessible without moving or lifting the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. All screens and/or lights must also be easily visible by competition personnel to assist in diagnosing {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} problems.
  This rule is in place to ensure the safety of both competitors and field staff. In the event that a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} needs to be quickly powered off—whether due to a malfunction, entanglement, or other safety concern—it is crucial that the power button remains easily accessible. This allows competitors and/or field personnel to safely {{LINK:#disablement}}Disable{{/LINK}} the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} without putting their hands near moving parts or other hazards inside the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. Additionally, keeping screens and indicator lights visible helps officials diagnose issues efficiently, minimizing downtime and ensuring a smooth competition experience.

rules.title: Firmware
rules.description: Firmware
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R10>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Firmware.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must use VEXos version 2.2.1 or newer on Gen1 Brains, or VEXos version 1.0.8 or newer on Gen2 Brains. The latest firmware can be found at {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/firmware}}https://link.vex.com/firmware{{/LINK}}. Custom firmware modifications are not permitted.
  
a. The minimum version requirement is subject to change over the course of the season.
b. When the minimum version is updated, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} have a two week (14 calendar day) grace period from the time the minimum version is changed to update their firmware to the latest minimum version.
c. VEX Robotics reserves the right to deem any firmware update critical, and remove the allowable grace period.

c. VEX Robotics reserves the right to deem any firmware update critical, and remove the allowable grace period.

rules.title: Motors
rules.description: Motors
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R11>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Motors.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use up to six (6) VEX IQ Smart Motors.
  
a. Additional motors cannot be used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (even motors that aren’t connected).
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<R11>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Motors.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use up to six (6) VEX IQ Smart Motors.
  
a. Additional motors cannot be used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (even motors that aren’t connected).

rules.title: Batteries
rules.description: Batteries
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R12>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Batteries.{{/BOLD}} The only allowable sources of electrical power for a VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} are one (1) VEX IQ Robot Battery (first or second generation) or six (6) AA batteries via the Robot AA Battery Holder (228-3493).
  
a. Additional batteries cannot be used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (even batteries that aren’t connected).
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are permitted to have an external power source (such as a rechargeable battery pack) plugged into their VEX IQ Controller during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, provided that this power source is connected safely and does not violate any other rules (such as <GG1>). Note: Although it is legal, the Robot AA Battery Holder (228-3493) is not recommended for use in the VEX IQ Robotics Competition.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<R12>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Batteries.{{/BOLD}} The only allowable sources of electrical power for a VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} are one (1) VEX IQ Robot Battery (first or second generation) or six (6) AA batteries via the Robot AA Battery Holder (228-3493).
  
a. Additional batteries cannot be used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (even batteries that aren’t connected).
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are permitted to have an external power source (such as a rechargeable battery pack) plugged into their VEX IQ Controller during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, provided that this power source is connected safely and does not violate any other rules (such as <GG1>). Note: Although it is legal, the Robot AA Battery Holder (228-3493) is not recommended for use in the VEX IQ Robotics Competition.

rules.title: One Controller per Robot
rules.description: One Controller per Robot
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R13>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}One Controller per Robot.{{/BOLD}} No more than one (1) VEX IQ Controller may control a single {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
  
a. No physical or electrical modification of the Controller is allowed under any circumstances.

  
  i. Attachments which assist the {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} in holding or manipulating buttons/joysticks on the IQ Controller (including 3D-printed controller attachments) are permitted, provided that they do not involve direct physical or electrical modification of the Controller itself.
b. No other methods of controlling the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (light, sound, etc.) are permissible.

  
  i. Using sensor feedback to augment {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} control (such as motor encoders or the Vision Sensor) is permitted.
  ii. See <RSC5>, <RSC6>, and <RSC7> for more information about operating the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} during {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:

  ii. See <RSC5>, <RSC6>, and <RSC7> for more information about operating the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} during {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Robots are built from the VEX IQ product line
rules.description: Robots are built from the VEX IQ product line
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R14>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots are built from the VEX IQ product line.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may be built ONLY from official {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} components from the VEX IQ product line, unless otherwise specifically noted within these rules.
  
a. Official VEX IQ products are ONLY available from VEX Robotics. To determine whether or not a product is “official” and legal for competition use, consult the following sources:

  
  i. {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/docs/viqc/legal-parts}}VEX IQ Robotics Competition Legal Parts Appendix{{/LINK}}
  ii. {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/docs/viqc/illegal-parts}}VEX IQ Robotics Competition Illegal Parts Appendix{{/LINK}}
  iii. {{LINK:http://www.vexiq.com/}}www.vexiq.com{{/LINK}}
b. If an inspector or {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} questions whether something is an official VEX IQ component, the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will be required to provide documentation that proves the component’s source. Such documentation may include receipts, part numbers, or other printed documentation.
c. Only VEX IQ components specifically designed for use in {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} construction are allowed. Using additional components outside their typical purpose is against the intent of the rule (i.e., please don’t try using VEX IQ apparel, {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} or event support materials, packaging, Field Elements, or other non-{{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} products on a VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}).
d. Official Robotics components from the VEX IQ product line that have been discontinued are still legal for {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} use. However, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must be aware of <R14b>.
e. Additional VEX IQ products that are released during the season are legal for use, unless otherwise noted on their product pages.
f. VEX IQ Smart Cables may only be used for connecting legal electronic devices to the VEX IQ Robot Brain.
  Note: A comprehensive list of legal VEX IQ parts can be found in the VEX IQ Robotics Competition Legal Parts Appendix, at {{LINK:https://www.vexrobotics.com/iq/competition/viqc-current-game}}https://www.vexrobotics.com/iq/competition/viqrc-current-game{{/LINK}}. This Appendix is updated as needed if/when new VEX IQ parts are released, and may not coincide with scheduled Game Manual updates.

rules.title: Prohibited items
rules.description: Prohibited items
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R15>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Prohibited items.{{/BOLD}} The following types of mechanisms and components are NOT allowed:
  
a. Those that could potentially damage Field Elements or {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}}.
b. Those that could potentially damage or entangle other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
c. Grease, oil, graphite, and/or any other lubricant or plastic additive.
d. Tape and/or any other material that adheres to or changes a legal part, other than non-functional decorations as permitted by <R17>.
e. Products from the VEX 123, VEX V5, VEX CTE, VEX EXP, Cortex, or VEXpro product lines, unless specifically allowed by a clause of <R16>.
f. Electrical components from the VEX Robotics by HEXBUG* product line.
g. Electrical components from the VEX GO product line.
h. 3D printed parts for any purpose, including non-functional decorations.
i. Additional illegal parts are listed in the VEX IQ Robotics Competition Illegal Parts Appendix, at {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/docs/viqrc/illegal-parts}}https://link.vex.com/docs/viqrc/illegal-parts{{/LINK}}. This Appendix is updated as needed, and may not coincide with scheduled Game Manual updates.

rules.title: Legal Non-VEX IQ components
rules.description: Legal Non-VEX IQ components
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R16>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Legal Non-VEX IQ components.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are allowed to use the following additional “non-VEX IQ” components:
  
a. Rubber bands that are identical in length and thickness to those included in the VEX IQ product line (#32, #64, #117B, & #170).
b. ⅛” metal shafts from the VEX V5 product line.
c. Other products from the VEX V5 product line that are also cross-listed as part of the VEX IQ product line are legal. A “cross-listed” product is one which can be found in both the VEX IQ and VEX V5 sections of the VEX Robotics website.
d. Mechanical/structural components from the VEX Robotics by HEXBUG* product line are legal for {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} construction.
e. Mechanical/structural components from the VEX GO product line are legal for {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} construction.
f. Aerosol-based cooling/freeze spray may be used to assist in cooling motors. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} using freeze spray or similar products in ways that may reasonably be deemed unsafe could be subject to <S1> {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}.
g. Cleaners, disinfectants, and/or sanitizers may be used to assist in cleaning {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, parts, components, etc. VEX Robotics recommends {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/360047126751-VEX-Product-Disinfecting-Guide}}these procedures{{/LINK}} for cleaning/disinfecting/sanitizing {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} parts.
h. Rubber bands no larger than 7.5” long and 0.25” wide.

rules.title: Decorations are allowed
rules.description: Decorations are allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R17>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Decorations are allowed.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may add non-functional decorations, provided that they do not affect {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} performance in any significant way or affect the outcome of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. These decorations must be in the spirit of the competition. Inspectors and {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} will have final say in what is considered “non-functional.” Unless otherwise specified below, non-functional decorations are governed by all standard {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules.
  
a. Decorations must be in the spirit of an educational competition.
b. To be considered “non-functional,” any decorations must be backed by legal materials that provide the same functionality. For example, a giant decal cannot be used to prevent {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} from falling out of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} unless it is backed by VEX IQ material. A simple way to check this is to determine if removing the decoration would impact the performance of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} in any way. {{BOLD}}* The HEXBUG brand is a registered trademark belonging to Spin Master Corp
{{/BOLD}}
c. The use of non-toxic paint is considered a legal non-functional decoration. However, any paint being used as an adhesive or to impact how tightly parts fit together would be classified as functional. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should be mindful of any non-functional decorations which could risk “distracting” an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} partner {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} Vision Sensor or other sensors.
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2862}}Q&A 2862{{/LINK}} - Using a marker to draw on the Robot for driver feedback is considered a functional decoration and isn’t legal {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Pneumatics
rules.description: Pneumatics
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R18>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Pneumatics.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} using parts from the VEX IQ Pneumatics Kit (228-8795) must satisfy all of the following criteria:
  
a. No more than two (2) Air Tanks, including any that aren’t connected.
b. No more than (1) Air Pump, including any that aren’t connected.
c. No additional parts that are not included in the VEX IQ Pneumatics Kit (e.g., unofficial tubing or fittings).
  There is no limit on the number of Pneumatic Cylinders or Pneumatic Solenoids that may be used, provided that no other rules are violated. There are no restrictions on running the Air Pump prior to (or during) {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.
  The intent of <R18a> is to limit {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} to the air pressure stored in two Air Tanks, as well as the normal working air pressure contained in any Pneumatic Cylinders and tubing on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not use other elements for the purposes of storing or generating air pressure.
 Using Pneumatic Cylinders or additional tubing solely for additional air storage is in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of the spirit of this rule. Similarly, using Pneumatic Cylinders and/or tubing without an actual pneumatic system (e.g., Air Tanks and/or a Air Pump) is also in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of the spirit of this rule.

rules.title: Modifications of parts
rules.description: Modifications of parts
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R19>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Modifications of parts.{{/BOLD}} Parts may NOT be modified unless specifically listed as an exception in this rule. Examples of illegal modifications include, but are not limited to, bending, cutting, sanding, gluing, lubricating, taping, and melting. The following exceptions are the only legal modifications of parts:
  
a. Cutting metal VEX IQ or VEX V5 shafts to custom lengths.
b. Bending parts which are intended to be flexible, such as string, rubber bands, or thin plastic sheets.
c. Cutting VEX IQ pneumatic tubing to custom lengths.
d. Tying knots to shorten or connect string or rubber bands.

rules.title: Standard rules apply in most cases
rules.description: Standard rules apply in most cases

rules.title: Scoring Robot Skills Matches
rules.description: Scoring Robot Skills Matches
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<RSC2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Scoring Robot Skills Matches.{{/BOLD}} For each {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are awarded a score based on the standard scoring rules.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<RSC2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Scoring Robot Skills Matches.{{/BOLD}} For each {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are awarded a score based on the standard scoring rules.

rules.title: Robot and Field setup for Skills Matches
rules.description: Robot and Field setup for Skills Matches
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robot and Field setup for Skills Matches.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} are set up the same as a {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} Match, with the following modifications:
  
a. The layout of {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} for a Mix & Match {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} differs from the layout for {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}, as shown in Figure RSC3-1.

  
  i. {{LINK:#pin}}Pins{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#beam}}Beams{{/LINK}} start in different locations on the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}.
  ii. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} receives one red {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} as a Preload.
  iii. Six {{LINK:#pin}}Pins{{/LINK}} (two red, two blue, and two orange) begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in the Red {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} for {{LINK:#load}}Loading{{/LINK}}.
b. The blue {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} is not used in {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} of any color may be introduced into the red {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} only. This includes any {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} that is reintroduced after leaving the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
c. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be placed in contact with the structure of the red {{LINK:#trianglegoal}}Triangle Goal{{/LINK}} at the beginning of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and after the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is reset under rule <GG10> or <RSC5>.
{{BOLD}}Figure RSC3-1:{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} Configuration for a VIQRC Mix and Match {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Loader and Driver differences
rules.description: Loader and Driver differences
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Loader and Driver differences.{{/BOLD}} All criteria listed in <SG6> apply as written (e.g., a Loader cannot put a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} into a {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} if there’s already a {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} there). {{LINK:#load}}Loading{{/LINK}} is modified as follows:
  
a. In both {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, any {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} who is not currently Driving the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may also serve as a Loader (i.e., a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may have one, two, or three Loaders at any given time).
b. The blue {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} is not used in {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} may only be introduced into the red {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}. This includes any {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} that is reintroduced after leaving the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
c. Any {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} that leaves the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} should be given to the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} human Loader for reintroduction at the red {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}}, regardless of that object’s color.
d. {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} must remain in the red {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} during {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, except when legally interacting with their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} per rule <GG10>.

rules.title: Handling Robots during an Autonomous Coding Skills Match
rules.description: Handling Robots during an Autonomous Coding Skills Match
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Handling Robots during an Autonomous Coding Skills Match.{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may handle their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} as many times as desired during an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}.
  
a. Upon handling the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, it must be immediately brought back to a legal starting position.

  
  i. {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} may reset or adjust the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} as desired from this position, including pressing buttons on the Robot Brain or activating sensors.
  ii. Any {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} being controlled by the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} while being handled must be removed from the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, and can be reintroduced by a Loader in accordance with <SG6> and <RSC4>. In the context of this rule, “controlled” implies that the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} was manipulating the {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}}, and not simply touching it. For example, if the {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} would move with the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} either vertically or while turning, then the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is “controlling” that {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}}. No other {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} should be moved or adjusted.
  iii. As described in rule <S1>, {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} cannot step into the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} at any time during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. If the {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} cannot reach the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} due to the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} being in the center of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, they may ask the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} to pick up the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and hand it to the {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} for placement according to the conditions above. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} does not get a new {{LINK:#preload}}Preload{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} during this reset.
b. During an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} may move freely around the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, and are not restricted to the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} when not handling their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. The rest of <GG1>, which states that {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are not allowed to use any communication devices during their {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, still applies.
  ii. An intent of this exception is to permit {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} who wish to “stage” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} handling during an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} to do so without excessive running back and forth to the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}}. This rule is an explicit exception to rules <GG4> and the Violation Note for <GG10>, and may be used as part of a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} strategy for {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.
 {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}} are still governed by <GG4> & the Violation Note for <GG10>, especially for strategic {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2883}}Q&A 2883{{/LINK}} - If a Pin is accidentally knocked off of a Starting Pin Support during a legal Robot reset, a Head Referee should put it back
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2974}}Q&A 2974{{/LINK}} - Alignment aids are prohibited during Robot resets {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Handling Robots during an Autonomous Coding Skills Match.{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may handle their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} as many times as desired during an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}.
  
a. Upon handling the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, it must be immediately brought back to a legal starting position.

  
  i. {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} may reset or adjust the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} as desired from this position, including pressing buttons on the Robot Brain or activating sensors.
  ii. Any {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} being controlled by the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} while being handled must be removed from the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, and can be reintroduced by a Loader in accordance with <SG6> and <RSC4>. No other {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} should be moved or adjusted.
  iii. As described in rule <S1>, {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} cannot step into the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} at any time during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. If the {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} cannot reach the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} due to the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} being in the center of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, they may ask the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} to pick up the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and hand it to the {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} for placement according to the conditions above. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} does not get a new {{LINK:#preload}}Preload{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} during this reset.
b. During an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} may move freely around the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, and are not restricted to the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} when not handling their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.

An intent of this exception is to permit {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} who wish to “stage” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} handling during an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} to do so without excessive running back and forth to the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}}.  {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}} are still governed by <GG4> & the Violation Note for <GG10>, especially for strategic {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2883}}Q&A 2883{{/LINK}} - If a Pin is accidentally knocked off of a Starting Pin Support during a legal Robot reset, a Head Referee should put it back
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2974}}Q&A 2974{{/LINK}} - Alignment aids are prohibited during Robot resets {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Starting an Autonomous Coding Skills Match
rules.description: Starting an Autonomous Coding Skills Match
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Starting an Autonomous Coding Skills Match.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} must start a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} routine by pressing a button on the Robot Brain or manually activating a sensor. Because there is no VEX IQ Controller hand-off, only one (1) {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} is required for an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} (though {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may still have two (2) if desired).
  
a. Pre-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} sensor calibration is considered part of the standard pre-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} setup time (i.e., the time when the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} would typically be turning on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, moving any mechanisms to their desired legal start position, etc.).
b. Pressing a button on the VEX IQ Controller to begin the routine is not permitted. To avoid any confusion, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are advised not to bring controllers to {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}}. In accordance with <GG3>, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should be mindful of event schedules and set their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} up as promptly as possible. The definition of “prompt” is at the discretion of the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, and could depend on things like how much time is left for the Skills Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}(s) to be open, how many {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are waiting in line, etc. As a general guideline, three seconds to calibrate a Gyro Sensor would be acceptable, but three minutes to debug a program would not.

rules.title: Autonomous means “no humans
rules.description: Autonomous means “no humans
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Autonomous means “no humans.”{{/BOLD}} During an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are not permitted to activate any controls on their VEX IQ Controller, and cannot manually trigger sensors (including the Vision Sensor) in any way, even without touching them.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} cannot use VEX IQ Controllers for any purpose in {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, and are advised not to bring controllers to the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}. If there is a controller at the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}, it should be placed on the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} outside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} and remain untouched until after the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

If there is a controller at the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}, it should be placed on the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} outside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} and remain untouched until after the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Skills Stop Time
rules.description: Skills Stop Time
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Skills Stop Time.{{/BOLD}} If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} wishes to end their {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} early, they may elect to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}. This is used as a tiebreaker for {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} rankings. A {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} does not affect a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} score for a given {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} and field staff must agree prior to the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} on the signal that will be used to end the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early.
  
a. As noted in the definition of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}, the moment when the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} ends early is defined as the moment when the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} have come to a rest and the {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} provides the agreed upon visual and audio signal to the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}}.
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who intend to attempt a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} must “opt-in” by verbally confirming with the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}} prior to the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}. If no notification is given prior to the start of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, then the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} forfeits their option to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
c. This conversation should include informing the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}} which {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} will signal the stop. The {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} may only be ended early by a {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
d. The agreed-upon signal to stop the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} must be both verbal and visual, such as {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} crossing their arms in an “X” or placing their VEX IQ Controller on the ground.
e. It is recommended that the {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} also provides a verbal notice that they are approaching their {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}, such as counting out “3-2-1-stop.”
f. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} runs multiple {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} in a row, they must reconfirm their {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} choice with the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}} prior to each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
g. Any questions regarding a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} should be reviewed and settled immediately following the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. <T1> and <T3> apply to {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2888}}Q&A 2888{{/LINK}} - Example of how to use a Skills Stop Time in an Autonomous Coding Skills Match {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and robot ruling decisions during the competition
rules.description: Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and robot ruling decisions during the competition
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and robot ruling decisions during the competition.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} score the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and may serve as observers or advisers for the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}, but may not determine any {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} directly.
b. When issuing a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} to a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} should attempt to notify the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} as the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} occurs, and after the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} must provide the rule number of the specific rule that has been Violated and record the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} in the Match Anomaly Log.
c. {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} require additional escalation beyond the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} initial ruling, including (but not limited to) investigation by RECF representatives.
d. {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} may not overrule a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} gameplay or {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} decisions.
e. Every {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} must be watched by a certified {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} may only watch one {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} at a time; if multiple {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} are happening simultaneously on separate {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, each {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} must have its own {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must follow the rules in this game manual and the Q&A, and must make rulings consistent with the intent of the game manual and Q&A.
f. At a minimum, every {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} must be watched by a trained {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}}, who may only watch one {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} at a time. If multiple {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} are happening simultaneously on separate {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, each {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} must have its own {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}}. A certified {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} must be available at the event to explain a rule, {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}, or other penalty to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} as needed in support of the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} at skills {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}. Note from the VEX GDC: The rules contained in this Game Manual are written to be enforced by human {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. Many rules have “black-and-white” criteria that can be easily checked. However, some rulings will rely on a judgment call from this human {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. In these cases, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} will make their calls based on what they and the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} saw, what guidance is provided by their official support materials (the Game Manual and the Q&A), and most crucially, the context of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in question.
 The VEX IQ Robotics Competition does not have video replay, our {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} do not have absolute sensors to count scores, and most events do not have the resources for an extensive review conference between each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
 When an ambiguous rule results in a controversial call, there is a natural instinct to wonder what the “right” ruling “should have been,” or what the GDC “would have ruled.” This is ultimately an irrelevant question; our answer is that when a rule specifies “{{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion” (or similar), then the “right” call is the one made by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} in the moment. The VEX GDC designs games, and writes rules, with this expectation (constraint) in mind.

 When an ambiguous rule results in a controversial call, there is a natural instinct to wonder what the “right” ruling “should have been,” or what the GDC “would have ruled.” This is ultimately an irrelevant question; our answer is that when a rule specifies “{{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion” (or similar), then the “right” call is the one made by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} in the moment. The VEX GDC designs games, and writes rules, with this expectation (constraint) in mind.

rules.title: Head Referees must be qualified
rules.description: Head Referees must be qualified
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Head Referees must be qualified.{{/BOLD}} VEX IQ {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must have all of the following qualifications:
  
a. Be at least 16 years of age.
b. Be approved by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}.
c. Be an REC Foundation Certified VIQRC {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} for the current season. Visit {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/14527449656087-REC-Foundation-Volunteer-Certifications}}the REC Library{{/LINK}} for more details.
d. Cannot be the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} or a Judge Advisor for the event.
Note: {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} must be at least 15 years of age, and must be approved by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<T2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Head Referees must be qualified.{{/BOLD}} VEX IQ {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must have all of the following qualifications:

b. Be approved by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}.
c. Be an REC Foundation Certified VIQRC {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} for the current season. Visit {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/14527449656087-REC-Foundation-Volunteer-Certifications}}the REC Library{{/LINK}} for more details.
d. Cannot be the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} or a Judge Advisor for the event.
Note: {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} must be at least 15 years of age, and must be approved by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: The Drive Team Members are permitted to immediately appeal the Head Referee’s ruling
rules.description: The Drive Team Members are permitted to immediately appeal the Head Referee’s ruling
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}The Drive Team Members are permitted to immediately appeal the Head Referee’s ruling.{{/BOLD}} If {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} wish to dispute a score or ruling, they must stay in the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} until the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} talks with them. The {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} may choose to meet with the {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} at another location and/or at a later time so that the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} has time to reference materials or resources to help with the decision. Once the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} announces that their decision has been made final, the issue is over and no more appeals may be made (see rule <T1>).
  
a. Referees are not permitted to review any photo or video {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} recordings when determining a score or ruling.
b. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} are the only individuals permitted to explain a rule, {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} to the Teams in a {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. Teams should never consult other field personnel, including {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}}, regarding a ruling clarification. Communication and conflict resolution skills are an important life skill for {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to practice and learn. In VEX IQ Robotics Competitions, we expect {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to practice proper conflict resolution using the proper chain of command. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule may be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <G1> and/or the Code of Conduct.
  Some events may choose to utilize a “question box” or other designated location for discussions with {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. Offering a “question box” is within the discretion of the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, and may act as an alternate option for asking {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} to remain in the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} (although all other aspects of this rule apply).
 However, by using this alternate location, {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} acknowledge that they are forfeiting the opportunity to use any contextual information involving the specific state of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. For example, it is impossible to appeal whether a game element was scored or not if the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} has already been reset. If this information is pertinent to the appeal, {{LINK:#driveteammembers}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} should still remain in the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}}, and relocate to the “question box” once the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} has been made aware of the concern and/or any relevant context.

rules.title: The Event Partner has ultimate authority regarding all non-gameplay decisions during an event
rules.description: The Event Partner has ultimate authority regarding all non-gameplay decisions during an event
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}The Event Partner has ultimate authority regarding all non-gameplay decisions during an event.{{/BOLD}} The Game Manual is intended to provide a set of rules for successfully playing VIQRC Mix & Match; it is not intended to be an exhaustive compilation of guidelines for running a VEX IQ Robotics Competition event. Rules such as, but not limited to, the following examples are at the discretion of the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} and should be treated with the same respect as the Game Manual:
  
• Venue access
• Pit spaces
• Health and safety
• {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} registration and/or competition eligibility
• {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} conduct away from competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} This rule exists alongside <G1>, <S1>, and <G3>. Even though there isn’t a rule that says “do not steal from the concession stand,” it would still be within an {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} authority to remove a thief from the competition.

rules.title: Be prepared for minor field variance
rules.description: Be prepared for minor field variance
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Be prepared for minor field variance.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#fieldelements}}Field Element{{/LINK}} tolerances and {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} positions may vary from nominal by up to ±0.5” [12.7mm], unless otherwise specified. The rotation of {{LINK:#pin}}Pins{{/LINK}} is not specified. If a {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} is within tolerance, either on the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} or on a {{LINK:#startingpinsupport}}Starting Pin Support{{/LINK}}, it should not be adjusted before the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are encouraged to design their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} accordingly. Please make sure to check Appendix A for more specific nominal dimensions and tolerances.
  The {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} and Field Elements are designed to be assembled and disassembled multiple times each year. {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} store and transport {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} between events, and the individuals setting up the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} at one event may differ from those at the next. While every effort will be made to ensure minimal variance, Teams should expect that any {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} may be slightly different than another, and prepare accordingly. Just because something works on one {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} does not fully guarantee it will work on the next, and is not enough evidence alone to determine if a {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} is out of tolerance.

rules.title: Fields and Field Elements may be repaired at the Event Partner’s discretion
rules.description: Fields and Field Elements may be repaired at the Event Partner’s discretion
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fields and Field Elements may be repaired at the Event Partner’s discretion.{{/BOLD}} All competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} and other Field Elements at an event must be set up in accordance with the specifications in Appendix A and/or other applicable support materials. Minor aesthetic customizations or repairs are permitted, provided that they do not impact gameplay (see <T4>).
 Examples of permissible modifications include, but are not limited to:
  
a. Replacing a damaged or missing VEX IQ component with an identical part of any color.
b. Elevating the playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} off of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} (common heights are 10” to 24” [254mm to 609.6mm]).
c. Using off-the-shelf PVC to replace a damaged or missing pipe.
d. Using double-side tape to stick the edges of the templates to the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} in cases where the sheet pins don't adequately secure the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} templates; cannot be requested by {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} if the sheets pins are sufficient to secure the templates to the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}
e. Using (or not using) the included VEX IQ sheet attachment pins to mark object locations for field reset.
{{CALLOUT}} Significant Q&As:
  
• {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/VIQRC/2025-2026/QA/2801}}Q&A 2801{{/LINK}} - Accommodation requests for color-blindness and marked Pins {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:

rules.title: Fields at an event must be consistent with each other
rules.description: Fields at an event must be consistent with each other
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fields at an event must be consistent with each other.{{/BOLD}} There are many types of permissible aesthetic and/or logistical modifications that may be made to competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion. If an event has multiple {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, they must all incorporate the same permissible/applicable modifications. If an event has multiple Robot Skills Challenge Fields, they must all incorporate the same permissible/applicable modifications. For example, if one Teamwork Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} is elevated, then all {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} must be elevated to the same height.
 Examples of these modifications may include, but are not limited to:
  
• Elevating the playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} off of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} (common heights are 12” to 24” [30.5cm to 61cm])
• Field display monitors
• {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} decorations (e.g., LED lights, sponsor banners attached to risers)
 
• Using (or not using) the included VEX IQ sheet attachment pins to mark object locations for field reset. Note: If an event has dedicated {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} for {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, there is no requirement for them to have the same consistent modifications as the {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}. See <T16> for more details.
rules.completeText:
