rules.title: Qualification Matches will occur according to the official match schedule
rules.description: Qualification Matches will occur according to the official match schedule
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Qualification Matches will occur according to the official match schedule.{{/BOLD}} This schedule will indicate {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} partners, {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} times, and, if the event has multiple {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, which {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} each {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} will be played on.
 
a. {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}} may be included in the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} schedule at some events, but are not required. If {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}} are run, every effort will be made to equalize practice time for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}.
b. A {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} can only start before its scheduled time if all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, and assigned volunteers are at the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} and ready to play.
c. Any multi-division event must be approved by the REC Foundation RSM prior to the event, and divisions must be assigned in sequential order by {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number.
d. The official {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} schedule is subject to changes at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion.
e. If the RECF Regional Support Manager determines that a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} should be removed from further {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} at the event based on the outcome of a {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violation{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} can use the full-event {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} feature in TM to remove that {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} from all further {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} at the event. Their spots in later {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} will be replaced automatically by {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} from the end of the Match Schedule, and {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} will be removed from the end of the schedule as appropriate.

{{BOLD}}<T9> {{BOLD}}Each Team will be scheduled Qualification Matches as follows.{{/BOLD}}{{/BOLD}}
 
a. When in a tournament, the tournament must have a minimum of six (6) {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} per {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} at local qualifying events and eight (8) for a Championship event.
b. When in a league, there must be at least three (3) league ranking sessions, with at least one (1) week between sessions. Each session must have a minimum of two (2) {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} per {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. The suggested number of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} per {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for a standard league ranking session is four (4). {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} may choose to have {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} as part of their league finals session.
{{BOLD}}<T10> {{BOLD}}Teams are ranked by their average Qualification Match scores.{{/BOLD}}{{/BOLD}}
 
a. When in a tournament, every {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will be ranked based on the same number of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. For tournaments that have more than one (1) division, Teams will be ranked among all Teams in their specific division. Each division will have its own set of {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}. The winners of each division will then have an overall event Finals. Any multi-division event must be approved by the REC Foundation Regional Support Manager prior to the event, and divisions must be assigned in sequential order by {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number.
b. When in a league, every {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will be ranked based on the number of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} played. Teams that participate in less than 60% of the total {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} available will be ranked below Teams that participate in at least 60% of the total {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} available (e.g., if the league offers 3 ranking sessions with 4 {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} per {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, Teams that participate in 8 or more {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} will be ranked higher than Teams who participate in 7 or fewer {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}). Being a no-show to a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} that a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is scheduled in still constitutes participation for these calculations.
c. A certain number of a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} lowest {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} scores will be excluded from the rankings based on the quantity of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} plays. Excluded scores do not affect participation for leagues. “No show” {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}, as described in <GG2>, are not considered lowest scores for the purposes of calculating a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} ranking and will never be excluded from the calculation.

 
{{TABLE}}
Number of Qualification Matches per Team | Number of excluded Match scores
----+----
4-7 | 1
8-11 | 2
12-15 | 3
16+ | 4
{{/TABLE}}
 
d. In some cases, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will be asked to play an additional {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}. The extra {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will be identified on the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} schedule with an asterisk and will not impact the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} ranking (or participation for leagues). Teams are reminded that <G1> is always in effect and Teams are expected to behave as if the additional {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} counted.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<T8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Qualification Matches will occur according to the official match schedule.{{/BOLD}} This schedule will indicate {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} partners, {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} times, and, if the event has multiple {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, which {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} each {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} will be played on.

  i. For tournaments that have more than one (1) division, Teams will be ranked among all Teams in their specific division. Each division will have its own set of {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}. The winners of each division will then have an overall event Finals. Any multi-division event must be approved by the REC Foundation Regional Support Manager prior to the event, and divisions must be assigned in sequential order by {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number.
b. When in a league, every {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will be ranked based on the number of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} played. Teams that participate in less than 60% of the total {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} available will be ranked below Teams that participate in at least 60% of the total {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} available (e.g., if the league offers 3 ranking sessions with 4 {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} per {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, Teams that participate in 8 or more {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} will be ranked higher than Teams who participate in 7 or fewer {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}). Being a no-show to a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} that a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is scheduled in still constitutes participation for these calculations.
c. A certain number of a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} lowest {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} scores will be excluded from the rankings based on the quantity of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} plays. Excluded scores do not affect participation for leagues. “No show” {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}, as described in <GG2>, are not considered lowest scores for the purposes of calculating a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} ranking and will never be excluded from the calculation.

 
{{TABLE}}
Number of Qualification Matches per Team | Number of excluded Match scores
----+----
4-7 | 1
8-11 | 2
12-15 | 3
16+ | 4
{{/TABLE}}
 
d. In some cases, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will be asked to play an additional {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}. The extra {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will be identified on the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} schedule with an asterisk and will not impact the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} ranking (or participation for leagues). Teams are reminded that <G1> is always in effect and Teams are expected to behave as if the additional {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} counted.

rules.title: Qualification Match tiebreakers
rules.description: Qualification Match tiebreakers
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T11>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Qualification Match tiebreakers.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} rankings are determined throughout {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} by:
  
a. Removing the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} lowest score and comparing the new average score.
b. Removing the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} next lowest score and comparing the new average score (on through all scores).
c. If the Teams are still tied, the Teams will be sorted by random electronic draw.
rules.completeText:

c. If the Teams are still tied, the Teams will be sorted by random electronic draw.

rules.title: How Alliances are formed for Teamwork Matches
rules.description: How Alliances are formed for Teamwork Matches
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T12>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}How Alliances are formed for Teamwork Matches.{{/BOLD}} During each {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, two (2) Teams form an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} that will play on the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}.
 
a. {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} are randomly assigned by the tournament software.
b. {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} are assigned as follows based on Teams’ rankings after all {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} have concluded (see <T10>):

  
  i. The first- and second-ranked Teams form an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
  ii. The third- and fourth-ranked Teams form an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
  iii. And so on, until all Teams participating in {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}} have formed an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
c. If, due to the outcome of a {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violation{{/LINK}}, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} must be removed from the {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} schedule after Finals {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} have been formed (see clause e of <T8>), the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} can use the full-event {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} feature in TM to remove that {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} from their scheduled {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}}. Their spot in the Finals {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will be filled manually by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} by the next-highest-ranked {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that is not already in a Finals {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. If that {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is unavailable or unwilling to participate, the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} can manually select the next-highest-ranked and available {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. If no replacement {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is available, the remaining half of that {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will play alone.

rules.title: Teams playing in Finals Matches
rules.description: Teams playing in Finals Matches
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<T13>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Teams playing in Finals Matches.{{/BOLD}} The number of {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}, and therefore the number of Teams who will participate in {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}, is determined by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}. Events that qualify teams directly to the VEX Robotics World Championship must have a minimum of five (5) {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}} if there are ten (10) or more Teams in attendance.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<T13>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Teams playing in Finals Matches.{{/BOLD}} The number of {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}, and therefore the number of Teams who will participate in {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}, is determined by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}. Events that qualify teams directly to the VEX Robotics World Championship must have a minimum of five (5) {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}} if there are ten (10) or more Teams in attendance.

rules.title: Finals Match Schedule
rules.description: Finals Match Schedule
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T14>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Finals Match Schedule.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}} are played sequentially, starting with the lowest-ranked {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will participate in one (1) {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} with the highest {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} score is the {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} champion.
  
a. {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} are ranked by their {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} score. The highest-scoring {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} is in first place, the second-highest-scoring {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} is in second place, etc.
b. Ties for first place will result in a series of tiebreaker {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}, starting with the lower-seeded {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} with the highest tiebreaker {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} score will be declared the {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} champion.

  
  i. If the tiebreaker {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} scores are tied, the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} with the higher {{LINK:#matchstoptime}}Match Stop Time{{/LINK}} will be declared the winner.
  ii. If the {{LINK:#matchstoptime}}Match Stop Time{{/LINK}} is also tied, a second series of tiebreaker {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}} will be played. If this second series of tiebreaker {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} is also tied, then the higher-seeded {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will be declared the winner.
  iii. If there is a tie for a place other than first, the higher-seeded {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will receive the higher rank. Example 1: {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 6 and {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 3 are tied for first place. During the tiebreaker {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 6 scores 13 points and has a {{LINK:#matchstoptime}}Match Stop Time{{/LINK}} of 12 seconds. {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 3 scores 13 points and has a {{LINK:#matchstoptime}}Match Stop Time{{/LINK}} of 10 seconds. {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 6 is the {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} winner.
 Example 2: {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 4 and {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 5 are tied for third place. {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 4 is the third place winner and {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} 5 is the fourth place winner. In this way, the lower ranked {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} must “overcome” the higher ranked {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} in order to become the {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} champion.

rules.title: Skills Match Schedule
rules.description: Skills Match Schedule
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T15>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Skills Match Schedule.{{/BOLD}} Teams play {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} on a first-come, first-served basis. Each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will get the opportunity to play exactly three (3) {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}} and three (3) {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}} at each tournament and/or League Session.
 Teams should review the event agenda and their {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} schedule to determine when the best possible time is to complete their {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}. If the {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} area closes before a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} has completed all six (6) {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, but it is determined that there was adequate time given, then the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will automatically forfeit those unused {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.
 Details regarding logistics of Skills-Only Events can be found in the {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/5474199602071-Qualifying-Criteria-for-VIQRC-VRC-and-VEX-U-Events}}REC Foundation Qualifying Criteria{{/LINK}} document.
  
a. {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} are only available to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who participate in {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}, unless the event is an approved Skills-Only Event. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who participate in {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} during a specific League Ranking Session are the only {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who can participate in {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} at that session.
b. Skills scores recorded by ineligible {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be deleted from Tournament Manager before the event is finalized on RobotEvents.com.

rules.title: There is no requirement that Skills Challenge fields have the same consistent modifications as the Teamwork fields
rules.description: There is no requirement that Skills Challenge fields have the same consistent modifications as the Teamwork fields
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T16>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}There is no requirement that Skills Challenge fields have the same consistent modifications as the Teamwork fields.{{/BOLD}} For example, there is no requirement that {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} are elevated to the same height as {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}.
 In order to use non-conforming {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} for {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} (e.g. during lunch), the following steps should be taken:
  
• Teams must be informed that the {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} may have some differences from the {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} (e.g., they might be elevated).
• Teams must be given an opportunity to select which type of {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} they want to use, i.e. they cannot be required to use a {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} for any {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Skills Rankings at events
rules.description: Skills Rankings at events
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T17> {{BOLD}}Skills Rankings at events. Teams will be ranked at an event based on the following scores and tiebreakers: {{/BOLD}}{{/BOLD}}
  
a. Sum of highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score and highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} Score.
b. Highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
c. Second-highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
d. Second-highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
e. Highest sum of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} from a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., the {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} in point 1).
f. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in point 2).
g. Third-highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score
h. Third-highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
i. If the tie cannot be broken after all above criteria (i.e., both Teams have the exact same scores and {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} for each {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}}), then the following ordered criteria will be used to determine which {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} had the “best” {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}:

  
  i. Points for {{LINK:#stack}}Stacks{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#standoffgoal}}Standoff Goal{{/LINK}}
  ii. Points from {{LINK:#matchinggoal}}Matching Goal{{/LINK}} Bonus
  iii. Points for 2-color and 3-color {{LINK:#stack}}Stacks{{/LINK}}
j. If the tie still cannot be broken, the same process in the step above will be applied to the Teams’ highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.
k. If the tie still isn’t broken, the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} may choose to allow Teams to have one more deciding {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, or both Teams may be declared the winner.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<T17> {{BOLD}}Skills Rankings at events. Teams will be ranked at an event based on the following scores and tiebreakers: {{/BOLD}}{{/BOLD}}
  
a. Sum of highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score and highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} Score.
b. Highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
c. Second-highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
d. Second-highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
e. Highest sum of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} from a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., the {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} in point 1).
f. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in point 2).
g. Third-highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score
h. Third-highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
i. If the tie cannot be broken after all above criteria (i.e., both Teams have the exact same scores and {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} for each {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}}), then the following ordered criteria will be used to determine which {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} had the “best” {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}:

  
  i. Points for {{LINK:#stack}}Stacks{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#standoffgoal}}Standoff Goal{{/LINK}}
  ii. Points from {{LINK:#matchinggoal}}Matching Goal{{/LINK}} Bonus
  iii. Points for 2-color and 3-color {{LINK:#stack}}Stacks{{/LINK}}
j. If the tie still cannot be broken, the same process in the step above will be applied to the Teams’ highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.
k. If the tie still isn’t broken, the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} may choose to allow Teams to have one more deciding {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, or both Teams may be declared the winner.

rules.title: Skills Rankings globally
rules.description: Skills Rankings globally
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T18> {{BOLD}}Skills Rankings globally. Teams are ranked based on their Robot Skills scores from Tournaments and Leagues that upload results to RobotEvents.com, according to the following tiebreakers.{{/BOLD}}{{/BOLD}}
  
a. Highest Robot Skills score (combined {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} Score from a single event).
b. Highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score (from any event).
c. Highest sum of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} used for point 1.
d. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} used for point 2.
e. Highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score (from any event).
f. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score used in point 5.
g. Earliest posting of the highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.

  
  i. The first {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to post a score ranks ahead of other Teams that post the same score at a later time, all else being equal.
h. Earliest posting of the highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.

  
  i. The first {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to post a score ranks ahead of other Teams that post the same score at a later time, all else being equal.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<T18> {{BOLD}}Skills Rankings globally. Teams are ranked based on their Robot Skills scores from Tournaments and Leagues that upload results to RobotEvents.com, according to the following tiebreakers.{{/BOLD}}{{/BOLD}}
  
a. Highest Robot Skills score (combined {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} Score from a single event).
b. Highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score (from any event).
c. Highest sum of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} used for point 1.
d. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} used for point 2.
e. Highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score (from any event).
f. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score used in point 5.
g. Earliest posting of the highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.

  
  i. The first {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to post a score ranks ahead of other Teams that post the same score at a later time, all else being equal.
h. Earliest posting of the highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.

  
  i. The first {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to post a score ranks ahead of other Teams that post the same score at a later time, all else being equal.

rules.title: Robot Skills at League Events
rules.description: Robot Skills at League Events
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T19>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robot Skills at League Events.{{/BOLD}} At league events in which Teams may submit {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} scores across multiple sessions, the Robot Skills scores (combined highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} scores) used for rankings will be calculated from {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} within the same session.
 For example, consider the following scores for a hypothetical {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} across two league event sessions:
 This {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} would have a Robot Skills score of 72 for this event’s rankings, and their scores from Session 2 would be used for the event and global tiebreakers listed in <T17> and <T18>.
{{TABLE}}
 | Autonomous Coding Skills Match | Driving Skills Match | Robot Skills Score
----+-----+-----+----
Session 1 | 25 | 45 | 70
Session 2 | 30 | 42 | 72
{{/TABLE}}
rules.completeText:

 For example, consider the following scores for a hypothetical {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} across two league event sessions:
 This {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} would have a Robot Skills score of 72 for this event’s rankings, and their scores from Session 2 would be used for the event and global tiebreakers listed in <T17> and <T18>.
{{TABLE}}
 | Autonomous Coding Skills Match | Driving Skills Match | Robot Skills Score
----+-----+-----+----
Session 1 | 25 | 45 | 70
Session 2 | 30 | 42 | 72
{{/TABLE}}

generalDefinitions.definition: {{BOLD}}Adult{{/BOLD}} - Anyone who is not a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} or another defined term (e.g., {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}).

{{BOLD}}Alliance{{/BOLD}} - A pre-assigned grouping of two (2) {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that are paired together during a given {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/BOLD}} - An {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} consists of a sixty-second (one minute) {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. There is no {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} can elect to end an {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} early if they wish to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Autonomous Period{{/BOLD}} - A time period during which {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} operate and react only to sensor inputs and/or commands pre-programmed by the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} into the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} control system.

{{BOLD}}Disablement{{/BOLD}} - A penalty applied to a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for a safety {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. During {{LINK:#disablement}}Disablement{{/LINK}}, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is no longer allowed to operate their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, and the {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} will be asked to place their Controller down. A {{LINK:#disablement}}Disablement{{/LINK}} is not the same as a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Disqualification{{/BOLD}} - A penalty applied to a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for a rule {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} (see <GG6> for more details). If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} receives a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} will notify the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} of their {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. At the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion, repeated {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualifications{{/LINK}} for a single {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may lead to its {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} for the entire event.
{{BOLD}}Driver Controlled Period{{/BOLD}} - A time period during which {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} operate their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Driving Skills Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} consists of a sixty-second (one minute) {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}. There is no {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} can elect to end a {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} early as described in rule <RSC8> if they wish to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Event Partner{{/BOLD}} - The volunteer VEX IQ Robotics Competition tournament coordinator who serves as an overall manager for the volunteers, venue, event materials, and all other event considerations. {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} serve as the official liaison between the REC Foundation, the event volunteers, and event attendees.

{{BOLD}}Field{{/BOLD}} - The entire playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, being six (6) {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} tiles wide by eight (8) {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} tiles long (totaling forty-eight (48) {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} tiles), including the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Field Perimeter{{/BOLD}} - The outer part of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, made up of four (4) outside corners and twenty-four (24) straight sections.

{{BOLD}}Floor{{/BOLD}} - The interior flat part of the playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, made up of the forty-eight (48) {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} tiles that are within the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Head Referee{{/BOLD}} - A certified impartial volunteer responsible for enforcing the rules in this manual as written. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} are the only people who may discuss ruling interpretations or scoring questions with Teams at an event. Large events (e.g., Signature Events, World Championships, etc.) might include multiple {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion.

{{BOLD}}Major Violation{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} which results in a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}.

 
 
• Unless otherwise noted in a rule, all {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} are {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}}.
• If noted in the rule, egregious or strategic {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} or intentional actions that result in {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} may also be {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}}.
• Multiple {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} within a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} or tournament may escalate to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion or as specified in a rule.

{{BOLD}}Match{{/BOLD}} - A set time period, consisting of {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Periods{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Periods{{/LINK}}, during which {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} play a defined version of Mix & Match to earn points. See Sections 3 & 4.

{{BOLD}}Minor Violation{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} which does not result in a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}.

 
 
• Accidental, momentary, or otherwise non-{{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} are usually {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}}.
• {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} usually result in a verbal notification from the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, which should serve to inform the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that a rule is being Violated before it escalates to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Qualification Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} used to determine the event rankings.

{{BOLD}}Robot{{/BOLD}} - A machine that has passed inspection, designed by {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members to execute one or more tasks autonomously and/or by remote control from {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Robot Skills Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Skills Stop Time{{/BOLD}} - The time remaining in a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} when a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} ends the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early.
  
a. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} does not end the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early, they receive a default {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} of 0.
b. The moment when the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} ends early is defined as the moment when the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} have come to a rest and the {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} has provided the agreed upon visual and audio signal to the Referee. See <RSC8> for more details.
c. If a Tournament Manager display is being used for field timing, then the {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} is the time shown on the display when the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is ended early (i.e., in 1-second increments).
d. If a manual timer is being used that counts down to 0 with greater accuracy than 1-second increments, then the time shown on the timer should be rounded up to the nearest second. For example, if the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabled{{/LINK}} and the timer shows 25.2 seconds, then the {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} should be recorded as 26.

{{BOLD}}Student{{/BOLD}} - Anyone born after May 1, 2010 (i.e., who will be 15 or younger at VEX Worlds 2026). Eligibility may also be granted based on a disability that has delayed education by at least one year. {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} are the individuals who design, build, repair, and program the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} with minimal {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} assistance.

{{BOLD}}Team{{/BOLD}} - Three or more {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} make up a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. To participate in an official VEX IQ Robotics Competition event, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} must first register on RobotEvents.com and receive a VEX IQ Robotics Competition {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number. A {{LINK:#team}}Team's{{/LINK}} unique number identifies their organization and their {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} within that organization. Each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} must build their own {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, create their own code, and maintain their own Engineering Notebook if they choose to use one.
  
• A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is classified as an Elementary School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} if all members are {{LINK:#elementaryschoolstudent}}Elementary School Students{{/LINK}}.
• A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is classified as a Middle School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} if any member is a {{LINK:#middleschoolstudent}}Middle School Student{{/LINK}}, or if the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is made up of {{LINK:#elementaryschoolstudent}}Elementary School Students{{/LINK}} who declare themselves as “playing up” as {{LINK:#middleschoolstudent}}Middle School Students{{/LINK}} by registering their {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} as a Middle School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
• Once a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} has competed in an event as a Middle School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, that {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may not change to an Elementary School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for the remainder of the season. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} mistakenly registers as an Elementary School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} but is ineligible for that age group, their registration may be revised mid-season with RSM assistance; all prior qualifications for the season will be lost.
• {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may be associated with schools, community/youth organizations, or a group of neighborhood {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}. In the context of this Game Manual, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} contain three types of {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} roles related to {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} build, design, and coding. See <G2> and <G4> for more information. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} may not fulfill any of these roles.

{{BOLD}}Violation{{/BOLD}} - The act of breaking a rule in the Game Manual.
 Some rules include {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} Notes in red italicized text to denote special circumstances or provide additional clarifications. If no {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} Notes are found in a given rule, then it should be assumed that the default definitions above apply.
 To determine whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} may have been {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}}, check whether the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} directly contributed to increasing the score of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. If it did not increase the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} score, then the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} was not {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}}, and it was very likely a {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}}.

gameSpecificDefinitions.definition: {{BOLD}}Beam{{/BOLD}} - A gray, roughly rectangular object with a size of 9.88” (251mm) x 4.88” (124mm) x 1.97” (50mm) and a weight of approximately 6 ounces (170g).

{{BOLD}}Builder{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}(s) on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who assemble(s) the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are permitted to teach the {{LINK:#builder}}Builder{{/LINK}}(s) associated concepts, but should never work on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Cleared{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#startingpin}}Starting Pin{{/LINK}} status. See <SC7>.

{{BOLD}}Coder{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}(s) on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who write(s) the computer code that is downloaded onto the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are permitted to teach the {{LINK:#coder}}Coder{{/LINK}}(s) associated concepts, but should never work on the code that goes on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Connected{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Object{{/LINK}} status. See <SC3>.

{{BOLD}}Designer{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}(s) on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who design(s) the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to be built for competition. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are permitted to teach the {{LINK:#designer}}Designer{{/LINK}}(s) associated concepts, but should never work on the design of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Drive Team Members{{/BOLD}} - The two {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} and one Loader who participate in a given {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} as representatives of their {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. No {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} may fill more than one role on a Drive Team in the same {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  
• {{LINK:#driver}}Driver{{/LINK}} 1 - Drives the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} until the mid-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} Driver switch (see <GG11>)
• Driver 2 - Drives the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} after the mid-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} Driver switch (see <GG11>)
• Loader - Introduces {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} into the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} for the whole {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}

{{BOLD}}Driver{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} member who stands in the {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Station{{/LINK}} and is responsible for operating and controlling that {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. Up to two {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members may fulfill this role in a given {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (see <GG1>), and there is no requirement that the same {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} serve as {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} in multiple {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Driver Station{{/BOLD}} - A region beside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} where the {{LINK:#driver}}Drivers{{/LINK}} must remain during their {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} unless legally interacting with their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#driverstation}}Driver Stations{{/LINK}} are represented by the red and blue lines in Figure FO-3.

{{BOLD}}Elementary School Student{{/BOLD}} – Any {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} born after May 1, 2013 (i.e., who will be 12 or younger at VEX Worlds 2026). {{LINK:#elementaryschoolstudent}}Elementary School Students{{/LINK}} may “play up” and compete as {{LINK:#middleschoolstudent}}Middle School Students{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Finals Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} used to determine the {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} champions.

{{BOLD}}Floor Goal{{/BOLD}} - A segment of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} in the center of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} surrounding the {{LINK:#standoffgoal}}Standoff Goal{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#floorgoal}}Floor Goal{{/LINK}} is defined by the center, white-outlined portion of the PET sheet connected to the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} underneath the {{LINK:#standoffgoal}}Standoff Goal{{/LINK}}, and includes that white line. The {{LINK:#floorgoal}}Floor Goal{{/LINK}} can hold a maximum of four (4) {{LINK:#placed}}Placed{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#stack}}Stacks{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Game Design Committee (GDC){{/BOLD}} - The creators of VIQRC Mix & Match, and authors of this Game Manual. The GDC is the only official source for rules clarifications and Q&A responses; see Section 1.
{{BOLD}}Goal{{/BOLD}} - A place where {{LINK:#stack}}Stacks{{/LINK}} can be {{LINK:#placed}}Placed{{/LINK}}. There are four (4) varieties of {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} in VIQRC Mix & Match:

{{BOLD}}License Plate{{/BOLD}} - A physical component on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that displays the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} VEX IQ Robotics Competition number. Each {{LINK:#licenseplate}}License Plate{{/LINK}} must have a length and height of 3.5” x 1.5” (88.9mm x 38.1mm) and must not exceed a width of 0.25” (6.35mm) per <R6>.
{{BOLD}}Load{{/BOLD}} - The act of legally introducing a {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#beam}}Beam{{/LINK}} into the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}. See <SG6>.

{{BOLD}}Load Zone{{/BOLD}} - A red or blue VEX IQ beam that is attached to the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} with VEX IQ pins. The {{LINK:#loadzone}}Load Zone{{/LINK}} is intended to receive {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} from a human Loader.

{{BOLD}}Match Stop Time{{/BOLD}} - The time remaining (i.e., displayed on the timer or audience display) in a tiebreaker {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Match{{/LINK}} (which only occurs for 1st place) when an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} ends the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early by placing their controllers on the ground. The {{LINK:#matchstoptime}}Match Stop Time{{/LINK}} is rounded down to the nearest even number. For example, if controllers are set down when the displayed time is 13 seconds, the {{LINK:#matchstoptime}}Match Stop Time{{/LINK}} is recorded as 12 seconds. If an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} does not finish the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early, they receive a default {{LINK:#matchstoptime}}Match Stop Time{{/LINK}} of 0 seconds.
{{BOLD}}Matching Goal{{/BOLD}} - A scoring bonus. See <SC6>.
{{BOLD}}Middle School Student{{/BOLD}} - Any eligible {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} that is not an {{LINK:#elementaryschoolstudent}}Elementary School Student{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Pin{{/BOLD}} - An orange, blue, or red roughly cylindrical object with a diameter of approximately 3.15” (80mm), height of 4.5” (116mm), and a weight of approximately 2.5 ounces (71g).
{{BOLD}}Placed{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#stack}}Stack{{/LINK}} status. See <SC5>.

{{BOLD}}Practice Match{{/BOLD}} - A non-scored {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} used to provide time for Teams to get acquainted with the official playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} should not record or track standard gameplay {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} that occur during {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} that are egregious, unsportsmanlike and/or unsafe may be recorded and tracked at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Preload{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} that is {{LINK:#load}}Loaded{{/LINK}} into a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} prior to a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. See <SG5>.

{{BOLD}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/BOLD}} - A portion of the VEX IQ Robotics Competition. The {{LINK:#robotskillschallenge}}Robot Skills Challenge{{/LINK}} consists of {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}} as described in the General Definitions.

{{BOLD}}Score Affecting{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} which improves an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} score at the end of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

 
 
• Multiple {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} within a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} can cumulatively become {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}}.
• When evaluating whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} was {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} will focus primarily on any {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} actions that were directly related to the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
• Determining whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} was {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}} can only be done once the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is complete and the scores have been calculated. {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} carry over into {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}} unless otherwise specified within a rule.
• {{BOLD}}CoC-related Violation{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of a rule that relates to the RECF Code of Conduct and related processes. For VIQRC, the included rules are: <S2>, <G1>, <G2>, <G4>, <G5>, <R1> through <R4>, and {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} of <S1>.

  
  • Potential {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} should be brought to the attention of the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}, and resolved through the Code of Conduct Reporting Process in coordination with the {{LINK:#scoreaffecting}}Score Affecting{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, Judge Advisor, and RECF Regional Support Manager. Follow-up investigations are conducted by the RECF Rules & Conduct Committee (RCC). Penalties for confirmed {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} can range from single-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualifications{{/LINK}} to removal from the program. Event staff other than referees may review video footage and/or photographs from the event as part of {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violation{{/LINK}} investigations; these reviews cannot change gameplay rulings made by {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/BOLD}} - An impartial volunteer responsible for tallying scores at the end of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} do not make ruling interpretations, and should redirect any {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} questions regarding rules or scores to the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Scoring Object{{/BOLD}} - A plastic object that can be added to a {{LINK:#stack}}Stack{{/LINK}}. There are two types of {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}} in VIQRC Mix & Match.

{{BOLD}}Square Goal{{/BOLD}} - A segment of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} in a corner of the long side of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} opposite from the audience. Each {{LINK:#squaregoal}}Square Goal{{/LINK}} is bordered by the inside of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} and the inside edges of the VEX IQ pieces connected to the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#squaregoal}}Square Goal{{/LINK}} has a color, red or blue, determined by its VEX IQ pieces and PET sheet. Each {{LINK:#squaregoal}}Square Goal{{/LINK}} can hold a maximum of one (1) {{LINK:#placed}}Placed{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#stack}}Stack{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Stack{{/BOLD}} - A set of two or more {{LINK:#connected}}Connected{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scoringobject}}Scoring Objects{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Standoff Goal{{/BOLD}} - The orange plastic structure mounted to the center of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} with IQ pins or screws.
{{BOLD}}Starting Pin{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} that begins the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} on a {{LINK:#startingpinsupport}}Starting Pin Support{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Starting Pin Support{{/BOLD}} - One of four (4) red or blue structures, built out of VEX IQ parts, that is attached to the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#startingpinsupport}}Starting Pin Support{{/LINK}} holds a {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} of its color at the beginning of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and until the {{LINK:#pin}}Pin{{/LINK}} is removed by a Robot.

{{BOLD}}Teamwork Challenge{{/BOLD}} - A portion of the VEX IQ Robotics Competition. The {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} consists of {{LINK:#teamworkchallenge}}Teamwork Challenge{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#finalsmatch}}Finals Matches{{/LINK}}, and may include {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Triangle Goal{{/BOLD}} - A segment of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} in a corner of the long side of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} closest to the audience. Each {{LINK:#trianglegoal}}Triangle Goal{{/LINK}} is bordered by the inside of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} and the inside of the PVC pipe that spans the corner of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#trianglegoal}}Triangle Goal{{/LINK}} has a color, red or blue, determined by its VEX IQ pieces, PVC pipe, and PET sheet. Each {{LINK:#trianglegoal}}Triangle Goal{{/LINK}} can hold a maximum of three (3) {{LINK:#placed}}Placed{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#stack}}Stacks{{/LINK}}.
